summary: Removed obsolete NON_MATCHING build handling from the Makefile and CI workflows. The PR states this logic already did nothing after MUST_MATCH was removed from the codebase, so the build matrix and package publishing checks no longer exercise a redundant NON_MATCHING=1 mode.
what_changed: Removed NON_MATCHING ?= 0 from the Makefile variable defaults.
Changed the default Makefile checksum logic so SKIP_CHECK is now the only explicit bypass path shown in this hunk; the NON_MATCHING=1 checksum skip branch was deleted.
Removed NON_MATCHING=1 from the build-melee workflow matrix, leaving only GENERATE_MAP=1.
Removed the publish-packages workflow step named 'Try building Melee (NON_MATCHING=1)', including its separate output/non_matching directory and docker run with MAKE_FLAGS="NON_MATCHING=1".
smart_moves: Deleted the CI invocation of a mode that no longer had semantic effect, reducing redundant build time and avoiding misleading coverage.
Removed the Makefile checksum exception tied to NON_MATCHING instead of preserving dead compatibility behavior.
Kept the GENERATE_MAP=1 CI path intact, making the cleanup narrowly targeted rather than altering unrelated build validation.
decomp_lessons: When a decompilation guard such as MUST_MATCH is removed from the codebase, audit build flags and CI workflows for stale companion flags such as NON_MATCHING.
Avoid keeping historical non-matching build switches once they no longer affect source selection or matching behavior; stale flags can make CI appear to test a mode that no longer exists.
Checksum skipping should be controlled by an explicit build flag such as SKIP_CHECK rather than an obsolete matching-mode flag.
naming_conventions: NON_MATCHING was an uppercase Makefile flag used as NON_MATCHING=1, but this PR removes it as obsolete.
GENERATE_MAP remains an uppercase Makefile/CI flag used as GENERATE_MAP=1.
SKIP_CHECK remains the explicit checksum bypass flag in the Makefile.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-level matching tactics were present in the provided PR slice.
The matching-related tactic was repository hygiene: remove an obsolete NON_MATCHING build mode after MUST_MATCH was removed.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find PRs that removed MUST_MATCH from the codebase.
Search for any remaining NON_MATCHING references after PR 1239.
Find Makefile changes involving SKIP_CHECK and checksum validation.
Find CI workflow changes that modify MAKE_FLAGS or build matrix coverage.
